findings.title: Webhook signature verification absent — unauthenticated attack surface
findings.description: No HMAC verification for GitHub X-Hub-Signature-256 or Notion webhooks. Internet-facing endpoint can receive forged payloads that manipulate beads (single source of truth).

findings.title: Common ancestor store persistence unspecified — silent data loss on restart
findings.description: Three-way merge requires a persisted common ancestor. If in-memory only, daemon restart resets sync state causing silent data loss or false conflicts on every entity.

findings.title: No graceful shutdown — SIGTERM loses in-flight events permanently
findings.description: Docker SIGTERM kills goroutines immediately. Events in the bus channel are lost. GitHub already ACKed the webhooks and will not retry. Permanent invisible sync divergence on every deploy.

findings.title: No per-entity serialization — concurrent updates resolved by goroutine scheduling
findings.description: Two adapters updating the same entity without per-entity lock or logical clock results in nondeterministic outcomes.

findings.title: Event lacks routing context — FS adapter silently drops unresolvable events
findings.description: Canonical Event has no RoutingHints map. Events without cross-system IDs cause nil return in FS adapter, not dead-letter.

findings.title: No panic recovery contract for adapter goroutine pools
findings.description: Brainstorm assumes panic recovery exists but does not require recover() wrappers. A nil pointer dereference kills the entire daemon.

findings.title: Event bus backpressure unspecified — slow adapter blocks webhook handler
findings.description: Shared channel with synchronous sends means a slow Notion adapter blocks GitHub webhook processing, causing delivery timeouts.

findings.title: No async webhook processing contract — synchronous handler timeout risk
findings.description: Webhook handlers must return 2xx within 100ms. Synchronous processing causes GitHub to disable the endpoint.

findings.title: No delivery ID deduplication — at-least-once creates duplicate beads
findings.description: GitHub retries with same delivery ID. Without dedup store, retries create duplicate beads issues.

findings.title: LWW clock source unspecified — external timestamp skew causes silent overwrites
findings.description: Using GitHub/Notion server timestamps for LWW instead of interop receive-time means clock skew silently discards valid writes.

findings.title: Conflict resolution policy is global, not per-adapter-pair
findings.description: Beads-authoritative vs Notion-authoritative requires directional policies. A single global strategy is wrong for at least one pair.

findings.title: No structured conflict record — silent tiebreaking
findings.description: Unresolvable conflicts resolved silently without audit trail. User never knows data was overwritten.

findings.title: Adapter interface lacks behavioral contracts
findings.description: Start/Stop/HandleEvent/Emit specify type signatures only. No timeout, blocking, or lifecycle contracts.

findings.title: Identity mapping deferred — will require painful retrofit
findings.description: Using display names for identity causes silent splits on rename. Stable system-native IDs (GitHub numeric ID, Notion UUID) needed from day 1.

findings.title: No HandleEvent error taxonomy — hub cannot distinguish retry-safe from fatal
findings.description: Without typed errors (malformed vs transient vs conflict), the hub either retries forever or drops permanently.

findings.title: No health vs readiness distinction — crashed adapters invisible to Docker
findings.description: HTTP server stays alive while all adapters are dead. Docker sees healthy; webhooks are silently dropped.

findings.title: No recovery checkpoint — crash causes full re-processing
findings.description: Without per-adapter last-processed-event checkpoints, crash restart re-applies all recent syncs.

findings.title: MCP and webhook server lifecycle not coupled
findings.description: If webhook server crashes but MCP stays alive, Claude Code sessions see stale state with no indication of failure.

findings.title: Goroutine pool worker count unbounded
findings.description: Without worker pool caps, a webhook burst spawns unbounded goroutines, exhausting file descriptors and memory.

findings.title: Notion webhook reliability undocumented — no polling fallback
findings.description: Notion webhook delivery is less reliable than GitHub. Without periodic reconciliation poll, sync silently lags during Notion outages.

improvements.title: Document goroutine lifecycle state machine (Starting -> Running -> Draining -> Stopped)
Add adapter compliance test suite validating behavioral contracts
Add Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint (/metrics) for alerting
Add sync state visualization MCP tool for debugging divergence
Add dead letter queue for events that pass signature but fail processing
Version the Adapter interface explicitly with compatibility checks
